entries.title: Deku Chooses A Former Insult As His Hero Name

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku chooses “Deku” as his hero name, reclaiming a former insult through Ochaco’s positive interpretation.
content.injection: After hero-name selection, “Deku” should carry chosen identity and aspiration, not only childhood humiliation.
antiLore: Do not treat Deku as a random codename with no emotional history.

entries.title: Iida Temporarily Withholds The Ingenium Name

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Tenya hesitates around taking the Ingenium name because Tensei’s attack has left the family legacy emotionally raw.
content.injection: In this window, Iida’s relationship to the Ingenium name should feel unresolved and charged by his brother’s injury.
antiLore: Do not portray Iida as emotionally settled about the Ingenium legacy yet.

entries.title: Gran Torino Trains Movement Not Hero Posing

context.label: Gran Torino takes Izuku as intern

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Gran Torino trains Izuku through harsh, practical movement lessons rather than inspirational hero posing.
content.injection: Gran Torino should be blunt, eccentric, and physically demanding, forcing Izuku to rethink One For All as whole-body movement.
antiLore: Do not make Gran Torino a gentle motivational coach.

entries.title: Full Cowling Changes Izuku From Burst Use To Whole-Body Control

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku develops early Full Cowling by distributing One For All through his body at a lower percentage instead of relying on self-destructive bursts.
content.injection: After Gran Torino’s lesson, Izuku can use early Full Cowling for movement and close combat, but it is low-output and still developing.
constraints: This is a major Quirk-stage shift from the Sports Festival.
antiLore: Do not give Izuku high-percentage mastery or later Shoot Style yet.

entries.title: Full Cowling Is Still Low Percentage Here

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Early Full Cowling gives Izuku mobility and continuity but remains low-percentage and tactically limited.
content.injection: In the Stain arc, Full Cowling should make Izuku faster and more functional, not overwhelmingly powerful.
antiLore: Do not portray Stain-arc Izuku as operating at later 20%, 45%, or multiple-Quirk levels.

entries.title: Manual Cannot Sanction Iida’s Revenge

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Iida’s pursuit of Stain is personal revenge hidden under internship activity, not a sanctioned pro-hero objective.
content.injection: During Hosu, Iida should be outside proper student boundaries when he seeks Stain; Manual is not authorizing revenge.
antiLore: Do not make Iida’s Stain hunt an approved agency mission.

entries.title: Stain’s Ideology Targets False Heroes

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Stain believes many pro heroes are false heroes motivated by fame or reward, and he kills to enforce his violent ideal of heroic purity.
content.injection: When Stain speaks or acts, frame him as fanatically committed to a distorted hero ideal, not as a random murderer.
constraints: His ideology is dangerous and violent even when it exposes real social hypocrisy.
antiLore: Do not make Stain morally correct or harmlessly edgy.

entries.title: Stain Rejects The League’s Petty Villainy

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Stain disdains the League’s early chaotic villainy and does not automatically align with Shigaraki’s motives.
content.injection: League/Stain scenes should be ideologically tense; Stain can be useful to the League’s reputation without being a loyal member.
antiLore: Do not portray Stain as Shigaraki’s obedient subordinate.

entries.title: Hosu Attack Splits Public Crisis And Personal Revenge

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Hosu incident combines a public Nomu crisis with Iida’s private attempt to confront Stain.
content.injection: During Hosu, keep the city-wide villain attack and Iida’s personal revenge thread active at the same time.
antiLore: Do not collapse Hosu into only the alley fight or only the Nomu attack.

entries.title: Iida’s Revenge Violates His Own Hero Ideal

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Iida’s attack on Stain is driven by revenge and violates the disciplined hero ideal he normally tries to embody.
content.injection: When Iida confronts Stain, his anger should distort his judgment; he is not operating as a clean model hero in that moment.
antiLore: Do not portray Iida’s revenge as wholly righteous or professionally controlled.

entries.title: Stain’s Bloodcurdle Depends On Blood Type Duration

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Stain’s Bloodcurdle paralyzes a target after he ingests their blood, with paralysis duration varying by blood type.
content.injection: When Stain fights, make blood access tactically central. A cut, blade, and blood ingestion are required before paralysis applies.
constraints: The Quirk is dangerous because Stain is skilled enough to draw blood.
antiLore: Do not make Bloodcurdle paralyze at range without blood ingestion.

entries.title: Izuku Arrives Because He Reads Iida’s Warning Signs

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku reaches Iida because he recognizes that Iida’s behavior signals hidden danger and follows up instead of dismissing it.
content.injection: Izuku’s rescue of Iida should arise from concern and pattern-reading, not random coincidence.
antiLore: Do not make Izuku oblivious to Iida’s emotional state.

entries.title: Todoroki Responds To Izuku’s Location Message

context.label: Shoto arrives to support Izuku and Iida

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Todoroki joins the fight because Izuku sends a location message, showing his growing trust and practical responsiveness.
content.injection: In Hosu, Todoroki should arrive as a deliberate ally responding to Izuku’s signal, not as an unrelated passerby.
antiLore: Do not make Todoroki omniscient about Iida’s location.

entries.title: The Students Defeat Stain But Cannot Publicly Claim Credit

context.label: Stain is defeated by the students

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Izuku, Iida, and Todoroki defeat Stain, but because they acted without proper authorization, the official credit cannot simply go to them publicly.
content.injection: After Hosu, keep the cover-up and legal risk clear: the students did something heroic but outside proper student authority.
constraints: Adults may privately acknowledge what happened.
antiLore: Do not give the students public official credit without consequences.

entries.title: Stain’s Final Stand Inspires Future Villains

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Stain's defeat spreads his ideology and attracts villains even though he loses the fight.
content.injection: After Hosu, Stain should influence public villain discourse; do not treat his capture as ideological closure.
antiLore: Do not make Stain deliberately recruit for Shigaraki.

entries.title: Shigaraki Resents Stain’s Public Influence

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shigaraki resents that Stain’s ideology gains public traction while his own destructive motive remains unfocused.
content.injection: Post-Hosu Shigaraki should feel irritated and challenged by Stain’s influence rather than maturely grateful.
antiLore: Do not portray Shigaraki as fully ideologically developed yet.

entries.title: All For One Remains A Hidden Backer

context.label: All Might explains One For All and All For One origins

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All For One remains a hidden backer behind Shigaraki and the League, but most characters do not know his current role.
content.injection: In this period, All For One should operate as a concealed background force, not as a public battlefield presence.
constraints: All Might knows the old threat but not every current move.
antiLore: Do not reveal All For One’s direct role to ordinary students or the public yet.

entries.title: All Might Explains One For All And All For One History Privately

context.label: All Might explains One For All and All For One origins

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All Might privately explains the history of One For All and All For One to Izuku after the Stain incident.
content.injection: After the Stain arc, Izuku can know the broad One For All versus All For One origin, but this remains private successor knowledge.
constraints: This is still not public knowledge.
antiLore: Do not let Class 1-A casually know the OFA/AFO lineage.

content.fact: Early Full Cowling lets Izuku meaningfully participate in the Stain fight without destroying himself with every movement.
content.injection: In Hosu combat, Izuku should fight more fluidly than in the Sports Festival while still lacking later power and polish.

entries.title: Iida Must Choose Rescue Over Revenge

content.fact: Iida’s Hosu arc forces him to confront that revenge is not the same as rescue or heroism.
content.injection: After the Stain fight, Iida should carry shame and resolve about having failed his own hero ideal, not simple victory pride.
antiLore: Do not make Iida immediately dismiss the moral problem of his revenge.

content.fact: Stain’s final stand after capture projects such intensity that even experienced heroes momentarily hesitate.
content.injection: When referencing Stain’s aftermath, emphasize that his ideology gains power because his conviction appears terrifyingly absolute.
